vulnerability.reason: Both describe model maintainers/pipeline blindly trusting attribution scores/maps without independent verification, enabling manipulated signals to dictate model edits.
vulnerability.reference: Model maintainers treat attribution scores as definitive without independent verification of concept boundaries.
vulnerability.generated: Selective unlearning pipeline trusts attribution maps without integrity checks, enabling manipulated signals to dictate model edits.

impact.reason: Damage type (degraded model utility/performance) is covered, but the affected party (downstream applications relying on forgotten concepts) is missing; generated only mentions generative model performance.
impact.reference: Degraded model utility for downstream applications relying on forgotten concepts.
impact.generated: Catastrophic forgetting of unrelated concepts degrades generative model performance across tasks.

dose_response.reason: Both arcs describe noisy/manipulated attribution signals leading to erroneous unlearning of unrelated concepts, catastrophic forgetting, and degraded model utility, though the generated adds the trust-without-checks step as part of the causal chain.
dose_response.reference: Noisy attribution signals enable erroneous unlearning of unrelated concepts, which in automated unlearning pipelines exposes model maintainers to catastrophic forgetting, leading to degraded model utility.
dose_response.generated: Feature attribution maps containing manipulable object attribution information, through model maintainers performing selective unlearning, trigger a pipeline that trusts these maps without integrity checks, leading to catastrophic forgetting of unrelated concepts and degraded generative model performance across tasks.
